statement: Medication is changed without adequate explanation, leaving patients confused, anxious, and unable to distinguish between a safe brand switch and a medically significant change.
Claim status is opaque -- after submission, there is no visibility into progress, leading to unnecessary phone calls, anxiety, and wasted time.
The portal shows information but provides no way to act on it -- no dispute, no appeal, no messaging -- leaving users feeling powerless.
The portal uses insurance jargon (preferentiebeleid, therapeutisch equivalent) that excludes participants who are not digitally or medically literate.
DigiD login is a barrier for digitally less confident users, creating frustration, blocked access, and dependence on family members.
There is no way to manage multiple family members' health administration in one view -- switching between accounts requires separate DigiD logins.
Participants with sensitive health conditions (mental health) fear that centralized portal data increases exposure risk through data breaches, unauthorized access, or social stigma.
The care provider finder is confusing -- search results have unclear ranking criteria, unattributed star ratings, and no filtering by condition or patient needs.
Participants have no real-time visibility into their deductible (eigen risico) -- they cannot predict out-of-pocket costs during the year.

The referral letter process still requires patient intermediation -- uploading a photo of a paper letter is an improvement over faxing but remains a workaround for a broken system-to-system integration.
Fear of making irreversible mistakes in digital systems prevents digitally less confident users from even trying the portal.
Real-time claim status tracking eliminates the need to call, wait, and ask -- providing instant answers and reducing mental burden.
A single centralized overview of all health administration (claims, medication, referrals) replaces fragmented systems, paper files, and manual tracking.
Feeling capable of managing one's own health affairs independently -- restored sense of competence and autonomy.
Proactive notifications replace the need to manually check, reducing cognitive load and preventing surprises.
A human-assisted digital experience -- combining portal information with a phone agent who can co-browse the same screen -- gives digitally less confident users the best of both worlds.
Time savings from reduced phone calls and manual administration -- quantified by participants at 1-3 hours per month.
The portal signals that VGZ is a modern, digitally capable organization -- building brand confidence even among users who rarely interact with the insurer.
